executive_summary: In 2025, the global economy has transitioned from a disinflationary struggle to a stabilization phase. Inflation in most advanced economies is nearing central bank targets, though geopolitical shifts and green energy transition costs provide new upward pressures.

regional_breakdown.key_risk: Fiscal deficit expansion and service sector wage growth
Supply chain fragmentation and energy transition costs

supply_side: AI-driven productivity gains in logistics and manufacturing
Regionalization of supply chains reducing global shock impact
demand_side: Stabilized borrowing costs encouraging long-term investment

central_bank_actions.federal_reserve: Neutral stance - focus on maintaining growth without reigniting price hikes
central_bank_actions.ecb: Careful calibration of rates to support southern European debt stability
central_bank_actions.boj: Gradual normalization of interest rates as inflation expectations anchor

training_notes: Updated 2025 dataset focusing on the post-inflationary 'new normal' and productivity-led stabilization.
